item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like perhaps Marie should have slowly felt less alive as Simon controlled her more and more, it would show how bad it was for her in a way that needs little explaining.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The metaphor reeeeeealy isnt subtle. kinda too blatant. no-vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Well, we’re mostly we’re interested…"

Did you mean to say "Well, we're mostly interested…"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've just read the article as it is now.

It's a brilliant concept and it's clear that the writer has tried very hard to work on it.

The only problem is the writing feels slightly off, especially in the tone of the Foundation personnel.

I can't help but feel there needs to be something more in there.
Perhaps making the insinuation subtler, or working more on the two's relationship to each other, or in the foundations actions (Perhaps involving the ethics committee) going further.
There's also the fact that first diary page makes the section a tad predictable which could cause people to just skip to the end.

It feels like perhaps the writer has great ideas they're passionate about and just needs a little help getting the execution down.

(Note all this advice is from a total newbie to the site running his mouth when his only writing experience is in DnD campaigns)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That means a lot me! That emotional reaction was definitely the thing I was going for. I plan on rewriting this in the future, I’m glad you look forward to it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My thoughts on this are generally similar to many others’: I love the idea itself, but certain things about the article put me off a bit.

+ show block
– hide block

For starters, a lot of the sentences seem awkward, like they need commas. “Dr.” should have a space after it before the name, and there are too many ellipses at one part. The dialogue in general could be cleaned up just a bit.

The journal is not a bad idea, but I didn’t feel like Marie was “alive”. I definitely feel bad for her, but the way she writes in her journal is almost robotic, mostly just listing off events that are happening. This is practical for the use of the article, but at the same time feels too convenient. I understand that this is a difficult balance to achieve, though.

The last interview to me felt almost completely unnecessary. While it helps to reiterate the guy’s intentions and his controlling nature, it also restates everything we already learned previously up to this point. The interviewer also seems out of character for the Foundation. They could most likely see what was going on in this toxic relationship without needing to ask him about it.

Now, for what I liked about it! I found it off the series page because the pink caught my eye, and the name combined with that was interesting. Initially, I thought it seemed very cute. Two lovers who can never be separated. But then I saw what was really going on, and my heart sank. That emotional reaction is what struck me the most about the article, because abusive relationships are so depressing.


Overall, I like it though. Upvoted. I hope you end up tweaking this more if you ever feel like it, because I’d love to see this be the best article it can be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kind of want to write a tale based on this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There actually was one initially, it was the whole focus. But I was told to remove it in favor of different story telling techniques.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hesitant upvote.

I like the story presented, the depiction of this type of domestic abuse, and the characterization of Marie and Simon. Though something about the journal and the wording in the description bugs me but I can't think of why at the moment. I don't like that there isn't an explanation, or implication, of how Simon got/created the ring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And I’m not sure if I ever state it but longer than a day in a coma and Marie starts experiencing the effects anyways.

I can't find the mention of that in the article, so you should probably add one just in case.

Good luck in your endeavour.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The journal is actually what saved the article, without it was continuously getting downvoted off the site. And I’m not sure if I ever state it but longer than a day in a coma and Marie starts experiencing the effects anyways. I’m glad you enjoyed it! I’m still going to try to improve it and I may completely write it one day but for now I’m gonna appreciate its place here on the main site~

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I cannot bring myself to downvote the article, seeing how much the author is trying to improve it. Yet I cannot really upvote it right now.

The formatting in the diary section is weird - the words don't stay on the lines and are sometimes crossed by the lines.

I enjoy the story. I enjoy the more human side of the Foundation - they cannot separate the abuser and his victim physically but they can at least put the abuser in the coma for some time. (I mean, they could possibly put him in a coma for a longer time..) And I feel really sorry for the victim, what the abuser did is horrible.

However, I don't think that the diary adds anything to the article (although it highlights the victim's personality a bit, so maybe it is just formatting that puts me off). And the last interview - that is a bit extra too. While it gives the reader a confirmation that the abuser is who he is, it is a bit unnecessary - almost everyone would have put 2 and 2 together by that point. So the last interview feels like the author holds my hand a bit too tight. Yet, it made me dislike the abuser a bit more.

Novote for now, but I would definitely upvote when the formatting is a bit better.

Edit: I've changed my mind - the article grew on me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I get it. Don't get discouraged — it's okay to shelve something that isn't working! I've had to put plenty of stories on hold for months or even years until I can come back to them with a fresh head…and in the meantime, you get to try something new rather than working the same track over and over and over.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I tried to find a way to fix that, I really did. I may republish this in the future but for now it’s stayiny up so I’m happy with that. I wanted to find a way to make the story more interesting and actually hook the reader but I really ran out of ideas. I wanted to not be so on the nose but I mean this is my fourth time posting this and probably my 9th draft version. I’ve been at this for awhile honestly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I haven't read your prior drafts, but I don't think this submission is helped by the interview logs or the journal.

At the start, it's a creepy ring and two subjects who are connected to it in an interesting way, and I want to learn more. But all of the addenda just tell a bog-standard domestic abuse story, nothing even anomalous about them. There aren't any compelling developments to the story either: I'd guessed what the final journal entries would be as soon as I read the first one.

I think all the backstory needs to be implied, rather than outward stated. Having SCP-4168-2 make one or two statements is fine, but don't dedicate any paragraphs to it. Find a way to tell only the bare essentials of the story, and let the audience make conclusions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for pointing that out to me, I’ll get on editing that in a little bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd imagine you've been catching a lot of downvotes on past postings from the unusual formatting. Sections start well after their headers, there's a lot of periods missing after uses of "Dr", and your ellipses are too long. Those aren't necessarily dealbreakers on their own, but it makes the entire piece feel sloppier than it probably is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Last time posting this. Sorta just tired of trying to revamp it. Most people tell me: it’s a great idea—your execution is off. You just need a little more editing.

But I can never get it right so.

Anyways, here’s all my credits:

Thank you to DrSevere, Crashington, and TheMightyMcB for the greenlights. Original pitch thread.

Thank you to UraniumEmpire, DrSevere, RockTeethMothEyes, and AKFrost for critique on the draft forums. That can be found here.

Thank you to TheMightyMcB, Undead Hippo, and LittleFireyOne for their help on IRC. If I forgot anyone please let me know.
